raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2503 will advance California’s climate goals by helping transit agencies electrify their fleets more rapidly.",
    "Electric trains are greener, faster, and more reliable.",
    "Train electrification is crucial to reducing greenhouse gas emissions in California and creating a more sustainable transit system.",
    "Caltrain’s Electrification Project is a major milestone for the state’s green transportation infrastructure.",
    "Caltrain is launching its fully electrified service between San Francisco and San Jose in September 2024.",
    "New electric trains will help reduce emissions, noise pollution, and improve air quality.",
    "Lawsuits using the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) bogged down Caltrain’s electrification efforts for years.",
    "AB 2503 will help eliminate barriers to future train electrification projects.",
    "The bill exempts zero-emission railway projects on existing rails from the requirements of CEQA.",
    "California has the goal of reducing greenhouse gas emissions to at least 85% below 1990 levels by 2045.",
    "The California Air Resources Board identifies reductions of Vehicles Miles Traveled (VMT) as a critical strategy to achieving California’s climate goals.",
    "Attaining VMT reductions includes investments in making transit a viable alternative to driving.",
    "Electric rail is a more sustainable alternative to diesel locomotives, which emit greenhouse gases.",
    "AB 2503 will enable transit agencies to advance California’s climate goals and improve rail service.",
    "Electric rail is seen as a solution for climate change, air pollution, and traffic issues.",
    "Electrification of railways is critical to meet California's climate goals."
  ]
}
```
